name: tool-presets
description: Standardized tool set definitions for Claude Code agents ensuring consistent tool access across similar agent types
capabilities: Defines dev-tools preset (Read, Write, Edit, Bash) for coding agents
Defines file-ops preset (Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob) for file manipulation
Defines analysis preset (Read, Grep, Glob, Bash) for code review agents
Defines research preset (Read, Write, WebSearch, WebFetch) for information gathering
Defines orchestration preset (Read, Write, Edit, Task, TodoWrite) for multi-agent coordination
Defines full-stack preset combining all tools for comprehensive agents
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: This skill is purely documentation containing markdown files that define tool preset configurations. All 58 static findings are false positives. The detected 'backticks' are markdown inline code formatting, not Ruby/shell command execution. The 'hardcoded URL' and 'C2 keywords' are metadata fields. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are hash values in JSON metadata. No executable code, scripts, or system access capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Different tasks require different tool access. This skill provides standardized tool preset definitions so you can quickly configure agents with appropriate capabilities for development, research, file operations, analysis, or orchestration tasks.
userTitle: Define consistent tool presets for Claude Code agents
limitations: This is a configuration skill only, not a standalone agent
Requires Claude Code or compatible platform to use the presets
Does not execute tasks itself, only defines tool access for other agents

useCases.title: Configure development agents
useCases.description: Set up coding agents with Read, Write, Edit, and Bash tools for development tasks.

useCases.title: Create file operation agents
useCases.description: Build documentation agents with file search and edit capabilities without shell access.

useCases.title: Orchestrate research workflows
useCases.description: Deploy coordinator agents that manage sub-agents for complex research projects.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Development Agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new agent using the dev-tools preset. It should have Read, Write, Edit, and Bash access for code development.

promptTemplates.title: Code Review Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up an agent with the analysis preset for code review. Include Read, Grep, Glob, and Bash for linter execution.

promptTemplates.title: Research Agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an agent using the research preset. It needs WebSearch and WebFetch for web research plus Read and Write for documentation.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Agent Coordinator
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure an orchestration preset agent that can launch sub-agents using Task and track progress with TodoWrite.

bestPractices: Start with minimal tool presets and expand only when needed for specific tasks
Match preset selection to the actual requirements of your use case
Review agent tool access periodically to ensure least-privilege principles
antiPatterns: Using full-stack preset when only specific tools are needed
Mixing tools from different presets arbitrarily
Ignoring the principle of least privilege for agent tool access

faq.question: Which platforms support these tool presets?
faq.answer: These presets work with Claude Code and Codex. The skill defines tool configurations that these platforms recognize.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of tools an agent can use?
faq.answer: Platform limits vary. Start with minimal presets and expand only when required for specific task capabilities.

faq.question: Can I create custom tool presets?
faq.answer: Yes. Define custom presets following the pattern in SKILL.md with your specific tool combinations.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using these presets?
faq.answer: Presets only define tool access. Actual data safety depends on platform permissions and agent behavior.

faq.question: Why is my agent not recognizing the preset?
faq.answer: Ensure the skill is added to your agent configuration and the preset name matches exactly as defined.

faq.question: How do these presets differ from custom tool configurations?
faq.answer: Presets provide standardized, tested configurations for common use cases. Custom configurations offer flexibility for specific needs.
